mechanism_narrative: An elevated federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, directly depressing the present value of stocks and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If the rank IC of FEDFUNDS level vs. 60-day forward returns is not consistently negative (below -0.03) over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above 5.5%, it signals labor market weakness that depresses consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: If, during periods when UNRATE > 5.5%, the median 5-day forward return is not negative (sign test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Rising retail sales (RSAFS) indicate robust consumer demand, which drives corporate earnings growth and lifts equity prices over the following month.
failure_condition: If the monthly change in RSAFS does not have a positive Spearman correlation (≥0.1) with next 20-day SPX returns over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: In the recovery phase after a recession, rising housing starts (HOUST) reflect improving household balance sheets and construction activity, which leads to positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE has declined by at least 1 percentage point from its 12-month peak and HOUST is increasing month-over-month
failure_condition: If, during periods meeting the trigger condition, the forward 20-day return is not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive monthly declines in industrial production (INDPRO) signal an industrial recession that reduces corporate earnings and leads to negative S&P 500 returns over the next five trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month decline for two consecutive months
failure_condition: If, after the trigger condition is met, the median 5-day return is not negative (sign test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: The joint condition of very low federal funds rates (≤0.5%) and rising month-over-month CPI (CPIAUCSL > 0.3%) indicates that monetary policy is becoming too accommodative for the inflation environment, forcing eventual tightening that depresses equity prices over the next month.
failure_condition: If, when both conditions hold simultaneously, the forward 20-day return is not significantly negative (one-sided p < 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) signals a strengthening labor market, which boosts household incomes and consumer spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive long-horizon equity returns.
failure_condition: If the 12-month change in PAYEMS does not have a positive correlation (Pearson > 0.1) with subsequent 60-day SPX returns over a 10-year window.

mechanism_narrative: When real GDP (GDPC1) grows at an annualized rate above 2.5% quarter-over-quarter, it indicates above-trend economic expansion that supports corporate earnings and drives positive equity returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: If, following quarters with GDP growth >2.5%, the median 60-day SPX return is not positive (sign test p > 0.05).
